extracted_via: automated retrieval against ClinicalTrials.gov API; reviewers verify and finalize
post_2015_filter: all 32 hits already post-2015 (Ultra was first WHO-endorsed 2017)
extraction_note: CT.gov has 32 trial registrations involving Xpert Ultra for TB (post-2015). None have 2x2 (TP/FP/FN/TN) data posted in CT.gov results sections — all DTA evidence is in published journal articles. Per spec §5 acceptance gate, the 2x2 extraction therefore comes from the PubMed-abstract sensitivity tier (T10), with CT.gov providing trial-design provenance (NCT linkage, sponsor, design, locations) for cross-checking against the publications. The 5 deep-dive entries below are the most-likely-to-have-DTA-data candidates; the remaining 27 hits are summarized in `summary_other_27`.

deep_dive_trials.title: A Multicentre Study of the Diagnostic Accuracy and Feasibility of the Xpert Ultra for Detection of TB and Rifampin Resistance in Adults Suspected of Having Pulmonary TB
deep_dive_trials.brief_title: Accuracy and Feasibility of Xpert Ultra
deep_dive_trials.sponsor: Foundation for Innovative New Diagnostics (FIND), Switzerland
deep_dive_trials.population: Adults ≥18 with clinical suspicion of pulmonary TB
deep_dive_trials.primary_outcome: Sensitivity and specificity of Xpert Ultra vs Xpert MTB/RIF for MTB detection (non-inferiority)
deep_dive_trials.notes: THE seminal Xpert Ultra DTA study. Multi-country FIND cohort. 2x2 counts available in published Table 2 (overall + smear-positive + smear-negative subgroups). Reference standard = sputum culture (LJ + MGIT).

deep_dive_trials.title: Xpert MTB/RIF Ultra Assay for the Detection of MTB in BAL for Pulmonary TB and EBUS-TBNA Samples for Mediastinal TB
deep_dive_trials.brief_title: TB PCR in BAL and EBUS-TBNA
interventions: Xpert MTB/RIF Ultra (BAL); Xpert MTB/RIF Ultra (EBUS-TBNA)
deep_dive_trials.population: Adults ≥18 suspected of pulmonary or mediastinal TB undergoing routine bronchoscopy
deep_dive_trials.primary_outcome: Diagnostic performance of Xpert Ultra in BAL and EBUS-TBNA samples vs culture/cytology
deep_dive_trials.publication_id: publication search needed in PubMed (T10) — likely 2024-2025
deep_dive_trials.notes: UK multi-centre study using BAL and EBUS samples. Specimen-type subgroup. Reference standard includes a composite clinical diagnosis category.

deep_dive_trials.title: Expanding Xpert Ultra Testing for TB Diagnosis Among HIV-positive Patients Admitted to Hospital in Africa
interventions: Concentrated urine + Xpert Ultra; Stool + Xpert Ultra
deep_dive_trials.reference_standard: Microbiologically confirmed TB (composite — multiple specimen tests)
deep_dive_trials.population: HIV-positive adults admitted to hospital wards in sub-Saharan Africa
deep_dive_trials.primary_outcome: Proportion microbiologically confirmed AND on TB treatment within 72h
deep_dive_trials.notes: RCT with treatment-initiation primary outcome — NOT a pure DTA study, but reports specimen-level Sens/Spec as secondary. Stool/urine alternative-specimen Sens/Spec available in publications.

deep_dive_trials.title: Multicentre Study to Evaluate the Diagnostic Accuracy of a Stool Processing Kit Combined With Xpert Ultra for Paediatric TB Diagnosis
deep_dive_trials.brief_title: Stool Processing Kit (SPK) Evaluation for Pediatric TB
deep_dive_trials.reference_standard: Microbiological confirmation on respiratory samples (sputum, NPA, gastric aspirate)
deep_dive_trials.population: Children ≤14 with clinical suspicion of pulmonary TB
deep_dive_trials.primary_outcome: Sensitivity and specificity of single Ultra/SPK on stool
deep_dive_trials.notes: Paediatric population. Stool-on-Ultra. Reference standard = respiratory-sample microbiology — different from adult sputum studies. SPECIMEN HETEROGENEITY: this is stool-Ultra, not sputum-Ultra. Whether to include in primary pool depends on review scope decision.

deep_dive_trials.title: Impact of Systematic Early Tuberculosis Detection Using Xpert Ultra in Children With Severe Pneumonia in High TB Burden Countries
deep_dive_trials.population: Children 2-59 months hospitalised with severe pneumonia
deep_dive_trials.primary_outcome: All-cause mortality at 12 weeks (NOT diagnostic accuracy)
deep_dive_trials.publication_id: Marcy 2023 — Lancet Glob Health (TB-Speed sub-study with Sens/Spec on NPA + stool)
deep_dive_trials.notes: Stepped-wedge cluster RCT with mortality outcome. DTA sub-study for Ultra-on-NPA and Ultra-on-stool published separately.

summary_other_27: Other 27 trials from the search returning hits include: NCT03187964 (POC Ultra in HIV+ initiating ART, n=1053, RCT), NCT06239337 (stool/urine Ultra paediatric, n=563, observational), NCT04203628 (stool processing methods + Ultra paediatric, n=215, INSERM), NCT07135622 (Ultra at primary health care RCT, n=778), NCT04781257 (TB contact early diagnosis, n=2100), NCT04303104 (POC active case finding RCT, n=3394, Cape Town), NCT06058065 (value of Ultra in suspected TB, n=200, Assiut), NCT04043390 (CRP+Truenat+Ultra, n=1100), NCT05117788 (Bioneer vs Ultra reference, n=2350, FIND), NCT04568954 (Truenat platform vs Ultra, n=3987, FIND), NCT05526885 (CAD4TB+CRP+Ultra, n=20000), NCT05285202 (community ACF/TPT, n=150000, Johns Hopkins), NCT05406453 (WGS for TB control, n=98362, Madagascar), NCT05139940 (AI TB screening Zambia, n=2432), NCT05845112 (START4ALL, n=14747, LSTM), NCT06401434 (CXR-AI EVALUATE, n=23835), NCT05452616 (HIV+TB ART initiation RCT, n=610), NCT04038632 (decentralized childhood TB, n=3106), NCT06700577 (MDR-TB Ultra+XDR, n=100, NOT YET RECRUITING), NCT04122404 (POC strategies HIV+TB, n=425), NCT05311423 (genital TB infertility, n=3000), NCT07248423 (Bangladesh/Cameroon/Kenya/Vietnam concurrent testing, NOT YET RECRUITING), NCT04700579 (lung microbiota active TB, n=150, observational), NCT03497195 (Zambia ACF, n=18194), NCT04121026 (PAANTHER TB algorithm validation, n=278), NCT03154320 (same-day testing HIV, n=500), NCT03356925 (POC Ultra RCT, n=1549, Stellenbosch). Most are RCTs of intervention strategies that incorporate Ultra rather than pure DTA studies; pure DTA evidence consolidates around NCT03072576 (Dorman 2018) and the FIND/INSERM-funded paediatric stool-Ultra cluster.
next_step: T10: PubMed abstract search to extract 2x2 (TP/FP/FN/TN) counts for primary tier (Dorman 2018, plus published reports of NCT04909385 and NCT04899076), and broader sensitivity-tier abstracts for systematic review evidence.
